articles.headline: Government Unveils 'India 2047 Economic Vision' Document, Emphasizing Green Growth and Digital Transformation
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today released its long-anticipated 'India 2047 Economic Vision' document, outlining a comprehensive roadmap for the nation's economic trajectory over the next two decades.
• Key pillars of the vision include achieving energy independence through renewable sources, leveraging digital public infrastructure for inclusive growth, and positioning India as a global manufacturing hub.
• The document stresses the importance of sustainable development, climate resilience, and equitable distribution of economic benefits across all sections of society.
• Focus areas include significant investments in education, healthcare, and skill development to harness India's demographic dividend.
• Public-private partnerships and regulatory reforms are highlighted as crucial for accelerating economic expansion and attracting foreign investment.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Judgment on Data Privacy in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today pronounced a significant ruling concerning the right to privacy in the context of rapidly evolving Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies and their data processing capabilities.
• The judgment clarifies the boundaries of state and private entity access to personal data, emphasizing the need for robust legal frameworks to protect individual autonomy and prevent algorithmic bias.
• It calls for a comprehensive data protection law that specifically addresses challenges posed by AI, including facial recognition, predictive policing, and automated decision-making.
• The Court reiterated that the right to privacy, a fundamental right under Article 21, extends to digital spaces and requires stricter scrutiny of data collection and usage practices by AI systems.
• This decision is expected to have far-reaching implications for technology companies, government surveillance programs, and the future development of AI in India.

articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Enhanced Trade and Maritime Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded today, with leaders affirming their commitment to strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
• Key outcomes included pledges to boost bilateral trade to new highs, focusing on supply chain resilience and digital economy integration.
• Discussions significantly revolved around maritime security in the Indo-Pacific, with joint statements emphasizing freedom of navigation and adherence to international law.
• India reiterated its support for ASEAN centrality in the regional architecture and proposed new initiatives for cooperation in green technology and sustainable development.
• Cultural exchanges and people-to-people connectivity also featured prominently, aiming to foster deeper understanding and collaboration.

articles.headline: Union Government Launches New National Policy on Sustainable Urban Waste Management
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today unveiled a comprehensive 'National Policy on Sustainable Urban Waste Management', aiming to revolutionize waste collection, segregation, and processing across Indian cities.
• The policy emphasizes a circular economy approach, focusing on waste-to-wealth initiatives, recycling, and reducing landfill dependency.
• It mandates stricter enforcement of segregation at source, promotes decentralized composting, and encourages the use of technology for waste tracking and monitoring.
• Financial incentives for urban local bodies (ULBs) and private players investing in advanced waste treatment plants are a key feature.
• The policy also includes provisions for public awareness campaigns and capacity building of sanitation workers, aligning with the Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) 2.0 objectives.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Generation Weather Satellite, Enhancing Climate Prediction Capabilities
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched a new state-of-the-art weather observation satellite from Sriharikota, marking a significant leap in India's meteorological capabilities.
• The satellite, equipped with advanced imaging radiometers and sounders, will provide high-resolution data for improved weather forecasting, disaster warning, and climate monitoring.
• It is designed to offer enhanced coverage of the Indian subcontinent and surrounding oceanic regions, crucial for predicting monsoons, cyclones, and extreme weather events more accurately.
• This mission reinforces India's self-reliance in space technology and its commitment to utilizing space applications for societal benefits.
• Data from the satellite is expected to aid various sectors including agriculture, shipping, and disaster management agencies, contributing to economic resilience.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Inflation Concerns, Signals Cautious Optimism for Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%, a move largely anticipated by market analysts.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to tame persistent retail inflation, particularly in food items, while supporting economic growth momentum.
• RBI Governor stated that while global headwinds remain, domestic demand continues to be resilient, signaling cautious optimism for the upcoming festive season and beyond.
• The MPC also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating that liquidity conditions will remain tight to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• Experts suggest that future rate decisions will be highly dependent on inflation trajectory, global crude oil prices, and the pace of economic recovery.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress of Border Infrastructure Projects Along Northern Frontiers
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence today conducted a high-level review of ongoing infrastructure development projects along India's northern borders, particularly focusing on connectivity and strategic access.
• The review highlighted significant progress in the construction of all-weather roads, tunnels, and bridges, aimed at improving troop mobility and logistics support for security forces.
• Emphasis was placed on accelerating projects in remote and high-altitude areas, crucial for maintaining effective vigilance and response capabilities.
• The government reiterated its commitment to modernizing border infrastructure to enhance national security and facilitate socio-economic development in border regions.
• Discussions also included leveraging advanced construction technologies and ensuring environmental sustainability in these ecologically sensitive zones.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Digital Learning Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020's implementation was conducted today, showcasing significant strides in developing digital learning infrastructure across the country.
• The Ministry of Education reported substantial progress in establishing e-content platforms, virtual labs, and digital libraries, particularly benefiting students in remote and underserved areas.
• The review also acknowledged challenges, including the need for enhanced teacher training in digital pedagogy and ensuring equitable access to devices and internet connectivity.
• States were urged to accelerate reforms in curriculum restructuring, multidisciplinary education, and vocational training as per NEP guidelines.
• The policy aims to transform India into a vibrant knowledge society and global knowledge superpower by making education more holistic, flexible, and accessible.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Celebrates 50+ Years: New Census Data Shows Steady Increase in Tiger Population in Key Reserves
articles.explainer:
• As Project Tiger completes over five decades of dedicated conservation efforts, the latest tiger census data, released today, indicates a continued positive trend in the population of big cats in several key tiger reserves across India.
• The report highlights the success of habitat restoration, anti-poaching measures, and community involvement programs in protecting tiger habitats.
• While overall numbers show an increase, concerns remain regarding habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict in certain regions.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to the 'Amrit Kaal Tiger Vision', aiming for sustainable tiger populations and robust ecological corridors.
• This achievement underscores India's leadership in wildlife conservation and its contribution to global biodiversity goals.

Biodiversity and Climate Change.

articles.headline: Parliament's Winter Session Likely to Focus on Uniform Civil Code Deliberations
articles.explainer:
• Ahead of the upcoming Winter Session of Parliament, sources indicate that discussions and potential legislative action on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) are expected to be a major highlight.
• The Law Commission's recent report on the UCC, submitted to the government, is likely to form the basis for initial debates among parliamentarians.
• Political parties are gearing up for intense deliberations, with proponents emphasizing gender justice and national integration, while opponents raise concerns about religious freedom and diversity.
• The government has expressed its commitment to implementing the UCC, citing its constitutional mandate under Article 44 of the Directive Principles of State Policy.
• Public consultations and expert opinions are anticipated to play a crucial role in shaping the legislative process surrounding this contentious issue.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Global Food Security Initiatives at FAO Conference
articles.explainer:
• India today reaffirmed its commitment to global food security, pledging enhanced support for international initiatives aimed at eradicating hunger and malnutrition, at the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) conference.
• The Indian delegation highlighted its successful domestic models, such as the National Food Security Act and various agricultural innovation programs, as examples for other developing nations.
• Discussions focused on climate-resilient agriculture, sustainable food systems, and leveraging technology to improve crop yields and reduce post-harvest losses.
• India called for greater international cooperation to address challenges posed by climate change, geopolitical conflicts, and supply chain disruptions affecting global food supplies.
• The commitment underscores India's growing role as a responsible global actor and its dedication to achieving Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 2: Zero Hunger.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Development of Advanced Battery Technology for Electric Vehicles
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists and engineers today announced a significant breakthrough in the indigenous development of advanced battery technology, specifically tailored for electric vehicles (EVs).
• The new battery chemistry promises higher energy density, faster charging capabilities, and extended lifespan compared to existing options, addressing key concerns for EV adoption in India.
• This innovation is expected to reduce India's reliance on imported battery cells and boost the 'Make in India' initiative in the burgeoning EV sector.
• Research focused on utilizing domestically available raw materials, aiming to lower production costs and strengthen supply chain resilience.
• The Ministry of Heavy Industries lauded the achievement, anticipating it will accelerate India's transition to sustainable mobility and contribute to energy security.

articles.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Critical Minerals and Rare Earth Processing
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today rolled out a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically targeted at boosting domestic processing and value addition of critical minerals and rare earth elements.
• The scheme aims to reduce India's import dependency on these strategic minerals, which are vital for high-tech industries, renewable energy, and defense applications.
• It offers financial incentives to companies investing in mining, refining, and manufacturing components from these minerals within India.
• The initiative is part of India's broader strategy to secure its supply chains for critical raw materials and enhance self-reliance in key strategic sectors.
• Experts believe this PLI scheme will attract significant private investment, foster technological innovation, and create a robust ecosystem for critical mineral processing.

articles.headline: Study Reveals Increasing Frequency of Extreme Weather Events in Coastal Regions, Calls for Enhanced Disaster Preparedness
articles.explainer:
• A new study published today by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM) highlights a worrying trend of increasing frequency and intensity of extreme weather events, particularly along India's vast coastline.
• The research points to rising sea levels, warmer ocean temperatures, and changing monsoon patterns as primary drivers behind more severe cyclones, coastal flooding, and storm surges.
• The study emphasizes the urgent need for enhanced disaster preparedness mechanisms, resilient infrastructure development, and early warning systems for coastal communities.
• It calls for integrated coastal zone management plans that incorporate climate change adaptation strategies and protect vulnerable ecosystems like mangroves and coral reefs.
• Policy recommendations include investing in climate-proof housing, improving evacuation protocols, and strengthening local governance for effective disaster response.

articles.headline: Uttarakhand Celebrates 25th Foundation Day with Focus on 'Devbhumi' Development and Sustainable Tourism
articles.explainer:
• Uttarakhand today celebrated its 25th Foundation Day, marking a quarter-century since its formation as a separate state from Uttar Pradesh on November 9, 2000.
• Chief Minister highlighted the state's journey of progress, emphasizing initiatives for sustainable development, eco-tourism, and preserving its unique cultural and ecological heritage.
• The focus remains on leveraging the state's 'Devbhumi' (Land of Gods) identity to promote spiritual and adventure tourism responsibly, ensuring environmental protection.
• New projects for rural connectivity, disaster resilient infrastructure, and harnessing hydropower potential were inaugurated.
• The occasion also served as a reminder of the challenges faced, particularly in managing natural disasters and ensuring equitable development across its diverse regions.

• The Ministry of Culture today announced a new nationwide scheme aimed at revitalizing traditional Indian crafts, preserving indigenous art forms, and economically empowering artisans.
• The initiative includes provisions for skill development, design innovation, market linkages, and digital promotion for various craft clusters across the country.
• Emphasis is placed on supporting women artisans and promoting geographical indication (GI) tagged products to ensure authenticity and fair remuneration.
• The scheme seeks to address challenges such as dwindling demand, lack of access to finance, and competition from mass-produced goods.
• It is part of a broader vision to celebrate India's rich cultural heritage, foster rural livelihoods, and boost the 'Vocal for Local' campaign.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Bilateral Talks on Indus Water Treaty, Focus on Technical Aspects
articles.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan held bilateral talks today on the technical aspects of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT), aiming to resolve outstanding issues related to hydropower projects.
• The meeting, held under the aegis of the Permanent Indus Commission, focused on sharing data and addressing concerns regarding the design and operation of run-of-the-river projects.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, a landmark agreement governing the sharing of Indus river waters.
• While the discussions were primarily technical, they are seen as a positive step in maintaining dialogue on a crucial shared resource amidst broader diplomatic complexities.
• The IWT has successfully survived several conflicts and tensions between the two nations, highlighting its resilience as a framework for water cooperation.

articles.headline: Ecologists Discover New Species of Amphibian in Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian ecologists today announced the discovery of a previously unknown species of amphibian in the highly biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The discovery highlights the immense and often unexplored biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's eight 'hottest' hotspots of biological diversity.
• The new species, a type of tree frog, was identified through extensive field surveys and genetic analysis, adding to the region's rich amphibian fauna.
• Scientists emphasize the urgent need for continued conservation efforts to protect these fragile ecosystems from threats like habitat loss, climate change, and pollution.
• This discovery underscores the importance of biodiversity research and its role in informing conservation policies for endangered species and ecosystems.

Biodiversity and Climate Change.

mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities.

highlights: Government unveils 'India 2047 Economic Vision' document, focusing on green growth and digital transformation for the next two decades.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'India 2047 Economic Vision' document, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to achieve complete energy independence primarily through fossil fuels by 2047.
2. It emphasizes leveraging digital public infrastructure for inclusive growth.
3. It outlines a roadmap for positioning India as a global manufacturing hub.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The vision document focuses on achieving energy independence through *renewable sources* and green growth, not primarily fossil fuels. Statements 2 and 3 are correct as the document emphasizes digital public infrastructure for inclusive growth and positioning India as a global manufacturing hub.

prelimsMocks.question:
The recent Supreme Court judgment on data privacy in the age of Artificial Intelligence primarily reiterates which of the following as a fundamental right?
(a) Right to Information
(b) Right to Privacy
(c) Right to Property
(d) Right to Education
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court's judgment on data privacy in the context of AI explicitly reiterated the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution, extending its scope to digital spaces and AI technologies.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020:
1. It replaced the 34-year-old National Policy on Education (1986).
2. A recent review highlighted significant progress in establishing digital learning infrastructure.
3. It primarily focuses on promoting traditional gurukul education systems.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 policy. Statement 2 is correct, as the review highlighted progress in digital learning infrastructure. Statement 3 is incorrect; NEP 2020 focuses on holistic, flexible, and multidisciplinary education, not primarily traditional gurukul systems, although it encourages Indian knowledge systems.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is the only Indian state that currently has a Uniform Civil Code (UCC)?
(a) Kerala
(b) Goa
(c) Uttarakhand
(d) Sikkim
prelimsMocks.explanation: Goa is the only Indian state that currently has a Uniform Civil Code, which it inherited from its Portuguese colonial past. Article 44 of the Indian Constitution states that the State shall endeavor to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent discovery of a new amphibian species, the Western Ghats is recognized globally as a:
(a) Major industrial corridor
(b) Significant agricultural plain
(c) Biodiversity hotspot
(d) Desert ecosystem
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Western Ghats is recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest' hotspots of biological diversity and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. It is known for its immense and often unexplored biodiversity, including many endemic species.

GS1.headline: Study Reveals Increasing Frequency of Extreme Weather Events in Coastal Regions, Calls for Enhanced Disaster Preparedness
GS1.explainer:
• A new study published today by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM) highlights a worrying trend of increasing frequency and intensity of extreme weather events, particularly along India's vast coastline.
• The research points to rising sea levels, warmer ocean temperatures, and changing monsoon patterns as primary drivers behind more severe cyclones, coastal flooding, and storm surges.
• The study emphasizes the urgent need for enhanced disaster preparedness mechanisms, resilient infrastructure development, and early warning systems for coastal communities.
• It calls for integrated coastal zone management plans that incorporate climate change adaptation strategies and protect vulnerable ecosystems like mangroves and coral reefs.
• Policy recommendations include investing in climate-proof housing, improving evacuation protocols, and strengthening local governance for effective disaster response.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Judgment on Data Privacy in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today pronounced a significant ruling concerning the right to privacy in the context of rapidly evolving Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies and their data processing capabilities.
• The judgment clarifies the boundaries of state and private entity access to personal data, emphasizing the need for robust legal frameworks to protect individual autonomy and prevent algorithmic bias.
• It calls for a comprehensive data protection law that specifically addresses challenges posed by AI, including facial recognition, predictive policing, and automated decision-making.
• The Court reiterated that the right to privacy, a fundamental right under Article 21, extends to digital spaces and requires stricter scrutiny of data collection and usage practices by AI systems.
• This decision is expected to have far-reaching implications for technology companies, government surveillance programs, and the future development of AI in India.

GS2.headline: Parliament's Winter Session Likely to Focus on Uniform Civil Code Deliberations
GS2.explainer:

• The Law Commission's recent report on the UCC, submitted to the government, is likely to form the basis for initial debates among parliamentarians.
• Political parties are gearing up for intense deliberations, with proponents emphasizing gender justice and national integration, while opponents raise concerns about religious freedom and diversity.
• The government has expressed its commitment to implementing the UCC, citing its constitutional mandate under Article 44 of the Directive Principles of State Policy.
• Public consultations and expert opinions are anticipated to play a crucial role in shaping the legislative process surrounding this contentious issue.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Global Food Security Initiatives at FAO Conference

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Bilateral Talks on Indus Water Treaty, Focus on Technical Aspects
GS2.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan held bilateral talks today on the technical aspects of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT), aiming to resolve outstanding issues related to hydropower projects.
• The meeting, held under the aegis of the Permanent Indus Commission, focused on sharing data and addressing concerns regarding the design and operation of run-of-the-river projects.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, a landmark agreement governing the sharing of Indus river waters.
• While the discussions were primarily technical, they are seen as a positive step in maintaining dialogue on a crucial shared resource amidst broader diplomatic complexities.
• The IWT has successfully survived several conflicts and tensions between the two nations, highlighting its resilience as a framework for water cooperation.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'India 2047 Economic Vision' Document, Emphasizing Green Growth and Digital Transformation
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today released its long-anticipated 'India 2047 Economic Vision' document, outlining a comprehensive roadmap for the nation's economic trajectory over the next two decades.
• Key pillars of the vision include achieving energy independence through renewable sources, leveraging digital public infrastructure for inclusive growth, and positioning India as a global manufacturing hub.
• The document stresses the importance of sustainable development, climate resilience, and equitable distribution of economic benefits across all sections of society.
• Focus areas include significant investments in education, healthcare, and skill development to harness India's demographic dividend.
• Public-private partnerships and regulatory reforms are highlighted as crucial for accelerating economic expansion and attracting foreign investment.

GS3.headline: Union Government Launches New National Policy on Sustainable Urban Waste Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today unveiled a comprehensive 'National Policy on Sustainable Urban Waste Management', aiming to revolutionize waste collection, segregation, and processing across Indian cities.
• The policy emphasizes a circular economy approach, focusing on waste-to-wealth initiatives, recycling, and reducing landfill dependency.
• It mandates stricter enforcement of segregation at source, promotes decentralized composting, and encourages the use of technology for waste tracking and monitoring.
• Financial incentives for urban local bodies (ULBs) and private players investing in advanced waste treatment plants are a key feature.
• The policy also includes provisions for public awareness campaigns and capacity building of sanitation workers, aligning with the Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) 2.0 objectives.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Generation Weather Satellite, Enhancing Climate Prediction Capabilities
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched a new state-of-the-art weather observation satellite from Sriharikota, marking a significant leap in India's meteorological capabilities.
• The satellite, equipped with advanced imaging radiometers and sounders, will provide high-resolution data for improved weather forecasting, disaster warning, and climate monitoring.
• It is designed to offer enhanced coverage of the Indian subcontinent and surrounding oceanic regions, crucial for predicting monsoons, cyclones, and extreme weather events more accurately.
• This mission reinforces India's self-reliance in space technology and its commitment to utilizing space applications for societal benefits.
• Data from the satellite is expected to aid various sectors including agriculture, shipping, and disaster management agencies, contributing to economic resilience.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Inflation Concerns, Signals Cautious Optimism for Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%, a move largely anticipated by market analysts.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to tame persistent retail inflation, particularly in food items, while supporting economic growth momentum.
• RBI Governor stated that while global headwinds remain, domestic demand continues to be resilient, signaling cautious optimism for the upcoming festive season and beyond.
• The MPC also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating that liquidity conditions will remain tight to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• Experts suggest that future rate decisions will be highly dependent on inflation trajectory, global crude oil prices, and the pace of economic recovery.

GS3.headline: Government Reviews Progress of Border Infrastructure Projects Along Northern Frontiers
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence today conducted a high-level review of ongoing infrastructure development projects along India's northern borders, particularly focusing on connectivity and strategic access.
• The review highlighted significant progress in the construction of all-weather roads, tunnels, and bridges, aimed at improving troop mobility and logistics support for security forces.
• Emphasis was placed on accelerating projects in remote and high-altitude areas, crucial for maintaining effective vigilance and response capabilities.
• The government reiterated its commitment to modernizing border infrastructure to enhance national security and facilitate socio-economic development in border regions.
• Discussions also included leveraging advanced construction technologies and ensuring environmental sustainability in these ecologically sensitive zones.

GS3.headline: Project Tiger Celebrates 50+ Years: New Census Data Shows Steady Increase in Tiger Population in Key Reserves
GS3.explainer:
• As Project Tiger completes over five decades of dedicated conservation efforts, the latest tiger census data, released today, indicates a continued positive trend in the population of big cats in several key tiger reserves across India.
• The report highlights the success of habitat restoration, anti-poaching measures, and community involvement programs in protecting tiger habitats.
• While overall numbers show an increase, concerns remain regarding habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict in certain regions.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to the 'Amrit Kaal Tiger Vision', aiming for sustainable tiger populations and robust ecological corridors.
• This achievement underscores India's leadership in wildlife conservation and its contribution to global biodiversity goals.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Development of Advanced Battery Technology for Electric Vehicles
GS3.explainer:
• Indian scientists and engineers today announced a significant breakthrough in the indigenous development of advanced battery technology, specifically tailored for electric vehicles (EVs).
• The new battery chemistry promises higher energy density, faster charging capabilities, and extended lifespan compared to existing options, addressing key concerns for EV adoption in India.
• This innovation is expected to reduce India's reliance on imported battery cells and boost the 'Make in India' initiative in the burgeoning EV sector.
• Research focused on utilizing domestically available raw materials, aiming to lower production costs and strengthen supply chain resilience.
• The Ministry of Heavy Industries lauded the achievement, anticipating it will accelerate India's transition to sustainable mobility and contribute to energy security.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Critical Minerals and Rare Earth Processing
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today rolled out a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically targeted at boosting domestic processing and value addition of critical minerals and rare earth elements.
• The scheme aims to reduce India's import dependency on these strategic minerals, which are vital for high-tech industries, renewable energy, and defense applications.
• It offers financial incentives to companies investing in mining, refining, and manufacturing components from these minerals within India.
• The initiative is part of India's broader strategy to secure its supply chains for critical raw materials and enhance self-reliance in key strategic sectors.
• Experts believe this PLI scheme will attract significant private investment, foster technological innovation, and create a robust ecosystem for critical mineral processing.

GS3.headline: Ecologists Discover New Species of Amphibian in Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian ecologists today announced the discovery of a previously unknown species of amphibian in the highly biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The discovery highlights the immense and often unexplored biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's eight 'hottest' hotspots of biological diversity.
• The new species, a type of tree frog, was identified through extensive field surveys and genetic analysis, adding to the region's rich amphibian fauna.
• Scientists emphasize the urgent need for continued conservation efforts to protect these fragile ecosystems from threats like habitat loss, climate change, and pollution.
• This discovery underscores the importance of biodiversity research and its role in informing conservation policies for endangered species and ecosystems.
